common.accessDenied: Access denied. This page is restricted to administrators.

merge.title: Merge Duplicates
merge.subtitle: Entries sharing the same GLN number are detected and can be merged.
merge.summary: {n} duplicate group(s) found. Select the record to keep.
merge.allCleanDesc: All organizations and practitioners have unique GLN numbers.
merge.name: Name
merge.orgHint: Non-kept organization is deleted; role references are remapped.
merge.practHint: Non-kept role is deleted; practitioner is removed if no remaining roles exist.

tasks.title: Open Tasks
tasks.subtitle: FHIR registry entries without a GLN number must be completed.
tasks.allDoneDesc: All organisations and practitioners have a valid GLN number.
tasks.orgsHint: Please assign a valid GLN number to these organisations.
tasks.practsHint: Please assign a valid GLN number to these practitioners.

patient.title: Patients
patient.name: Name
patient.mergeHint: Select two patients (first = target, second = source).
patient.mergeSelectFirst: Click first patient (target – will be kept).
patient.mergeSelectSecond: selected as target. Click second patient (will be merged).

orders.title: Orders
orders.tooltipDraft: Draft – not yet sent to lab. Can be edited or submitted.
orders.tooltipActive: Sent – waiting for lab to process. Can still be edited as long as the lab has not locked it.
orders.tooltipOnHold: On hold – lab has temporarily paused the order.
orders.tooltipCompleted: Completed – lab has finished processing. No further changes possible.
orders.tooltipRevoked: Revoked – order has been cancelled. No further changes possible.
orders.tooltipError: Entered in error – order was marked as erroneous.

profile.title: My Profile
profile.glnUnavailable: GLN lookup temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
profile.orgGlnNatHint: The practitioner is linked to this organisation via a PractitionerRole.
profile.orgGlnJurHint: This organisation is a sub-unit of the specified parent organisation (Organization.partOf).

order.title: Order entry
order.noMaterial: No material yet (derived from selected analyses)
order.readOnly: This order is locked and can no longer be edited.
order.inactivePatientHint: Orders cannot be created for inactive patients. Please reactivate the patient first.

befunde.title: Results
befunde.tooltipRegistered: Order received by lab, analysis not yet started.
befunde.tooltipPartial: Some results available, report not yet complete.
befunde.tooltipFinal: Report is complete and finally released.
befunde.tooltipAmended: Report was supplemented or changed after release.
befunde.tooltipCorrected: Report was corrected after release (error correction).
befunde.tooltipCancelled: Test was cancelled, no result available.

settings.title: Settings
settings.debugModeHelp: Shows additional diagnostic information in the user interface.
settings.logLevelHelp: Log level for the browser console (DevTools). Changes apply immediately.
settings.serverNote: Server settings can only be changed via environment variables (LOG_LEVEL, LOG_FILE, FHIR_BASE_URL). Restart the server afterwards.
settings.logsDesc: Download server logs or open the log viewer.
settings.logsNotConfigured: LOG_FILE is not configured — no log file available.
settings.logViewerDisabled: File logging is not active. Set LOG_FILE in the environment variables.
settings.envEditorHelp: Only approved variables are shown. Secrets (AUTH_SECRET, etc.) are hidden.
settings.envEditorSaved: Changes saved. Please restart the application or container to apply them.
settings.envEditorEmptyKey: Variable name must not be empty.
settings.envEditorRestartNote: Environment variables are loaded at application startup. Changes to .env.local take effect only after restarting the container. Hot-reloading is not supported.
settings.envEditorUnavailable: Not available in this environment. Manage environment variables via the deployment dashboard (e.g. Vercel).

envSchema.title: ENV Configuration
envSchema.subtitle: All environment variables with description, default and current value
envSchema.secretNote: Secret value — will not be shown after saving.
envSchema.clearNote: Leave empty to use the default value.
envSchema.lockedByEnvNote: This value is set by a system environment variable (e.g. docker-compose) and cannot be edited here.

auth.title: Sign in
auth.subtitle: z2Lab OrderEntry
auth.error503: Service unavailable. Please contact your administrator.
auth.error400: Invalid input. Please check your username and password.
auth.errorNetwork: Server unreachable. Please check your internet connection.
auth.errorDefault: Sign in failed. Please try again.
auth.glnHint: Your 13-digit GLN as a healthcare professional (optional)
auth.signupLocalSuccess: Account created locally on this device.
auth.orgGlnHint: 13-digit GLN of your institution (e.g. clinic, practice)
auth.orgGlnInvalid: Organisation GLN must contain exactly 13 digits.
auth.noOrgAccessDesc: Your account is not linked to an organisation. Please contact your administrator.
auth.forgotSubtitle: Enter your username and we will send you a reset link by email.
auth.resetEmailSent: If an account with a stored email address exists, a reset link has been sent. Please check your inbox.
auth.resetRequestError: Error sending the email. Please try again.
auth.resetSuccess: Password changed successfully. You can now log in.
auth.resetConfirmError: Error resetting password. The link may have expired.

idleTimeout.title: Session expiring
idleTimeout.subtitle: You have been inactive for a while.

apiPage.title: API Documentation
apiPage.subtitle: Endpoints, authentication and integration guide
apiPage.swaggerDesc: Explore all available endpoints directly in the browser. Test requests using your real credentials.
apiPage.swaggerOpen: Open Swagger UI in new tab
apiPage.authDesc: The API uses HMAC-SHA256 signed session cookies. Sign in first to receive a cookie, then send it with every subsequent request.
apiPage.authStep1: Sign in and save the cookie
apiPage.authNoteDesc: All admin endpoints (/api/users/*) require a session with role=admin. Requests without a valid session receive 401 (not authenticated) or 403 (not an admin).
apiPage.connectDesc: Examples for common use cases using cURL. Replace <host> with the server address.
apiPage.fhirDesc: The API does not store data directly. All data operations are forwarded as FHIR transaction bundles to the HAPI FHIR R4 server.
apiPage.wadlDesc: The Web Application Description Language (WADL) document describes all API resources and methods. The OpenAPI 3.0 spec is the primary source for both Swagger and WADL.

users.title: User management
users.syncNoPtype: FHIR sync requires a person type (NAT/JUR/PER) — edit the user and set a type first
users.ptypeRequired: Please select a person type (NAT, JUR or PER).
users.roleTypeRequired: Please select at least one role.

roles.title: Role management
roles.glnRequired: GLN is required for type NAT (Person / Practitioner)
roles.deleteConfirm: Really delete this role? It will be removed from the catalog.
roles.hintText: Roles are assigned as tags when creating/editing users. The code is transferred as PractitionerRole.code during FHIR sync.

fhirRegistry.title: FHIR Registry
fhirRegistry.subtitle: Create organisations, practitioners and users in the correct order
fhirRegistry.stepBanner: Mandatory order: ① Create organisation → ② Create practitioner → ③ Assign users
fhirRegistry.usersTab: Users are managed in the user management page and assigned to an organisation.
fhirRegistry.usersNote: Users must be assigned to an organisation.

system.orgAssignmentDesc: Your organisation access determines which patients and data you can see.
system.adminHint: As an administrator, you can assign organisations to users in the user management page.

authConfig.title: FHIR Auth Configuration
authConfig.desc: Controls how the server authenticates outbound requests to the FHIR backend. Credentials are configured via environment variables only.
authConfig.referenceDesc: Choose the appropriate method and set the corresponding ENV variables in .env.local or docker-compose.yml.
authConfig.securityNotice: Credentials (passwords, tokens, client secrets) must never be edited through the Admin UI. They must be set directly as environment variables.

logs.title: System Logs
logs.notConfiguredDesc: Set the LOG_FILE environment variable to a writable path inside the container to enable persistent logging.

orgs.title: Organizations
orgs.subtitle: Manage FHIR organizations and assign them to users
orgs.deleteConflict: Cannot delete this organization — it is still referenced by other records.
orgs.conflictBody: «{name}» is still used by the following resources:
orgs.deleteFailed: Delete failed. Please try again later.
orgs.name: Name
orgs.assignHint: Organizations can be assigned to users in
orgs.parentHint: Optional: This organization belongs to a parent organization (e.g. a department within a clinic).
orgs.locationNone: No locations found for this organisation.

mail.title: Mail Server Configuration
mail.desc: Configures outbound email delivery (SMTP, Gmail, OAuth2). Credentials are set exclusively via environment variables.
mail.referenceDesc: Select provider and authentication method to see the required ENV variables.
mail.securityNotice: Credentials (passwords, OAuth2 tokens, client secrets) must never be edited via the Admin UI. They must be set directly as environment variables (MAIL_PASSWORD, MAIL_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, MAIL_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN).
mail.serverPresetChoose: — Select a server or enter a custom one —
mail.hinConfiguredText: HIN is set up as the mail provider. Patient data and lab results are transmitted encrypted in compliance with nDSG.
mail.hinNdsgText: Under the revised Swiss Data Protection Act (nDSG, in force since 1 Sept. 2023), patient data and lab results must be transmitted encrypted. HIN (Health Info Net) is the recognised standard in Swiss healthcare.
mail.hinOption1: Infomaniak Mail + HIN certificate (recommended — SMTP-compatible, CHF 50–200/month)
mail.hinOption2: SeppMail HIN Connector (gateway solution, transparent to nodemailer)
mail.hinOption3: HIN Mail Gateway directly (enterprise licence, contact: hin.ch)
mail.hinMore: Without HIN or equivalent S/MIME encryption, patient data may only be sent by email with explicit written consent from the patient.
mail.smtpHelp: Use for internal hospital or laboratory SMTP servers and standard external providers. Supports STARTTLS (port 587) and SSL/TLS (port 465). Recommended for production environments.
mail.gmailHelp: Recommended for development and testing only — not suitable for medical production environments. Gmail has sending limits (500 emails/day) and is not a medically certified mail provider.

orgRules.title: Org Rules
orgRules.subtitle: Organisation-specific HL7 configuration, patient and case number prefixes, and order number overrides.
orgRules.empty: No org rules yet. Click «New Rule» to create one.
orgRules.searchHint: Minimum 2 characters. Searches the FHIR server by GLN or name.
orgRules.numberConfigHint: Leave empty to use the global ENV defaults.
orgRules.mappingHint: Map external department codes to a service type (MIBI / ROUTINE / POC).

pool.title: Number Pool
pool.subtitle: Pre-reserved order numbers used as fallback when Orchestra is unavailable.
pool.thresholdHint: An email is sent when the pool drops below each threshold (anti-spam: once per level until the pool is refilled). Rule: errorAt < warnAt < infoAt.
pool.confirmDelete: Really delete this number from the pool?

error.invalidMsg: The link you followed is invalid or has been tampered with. Please request a new link from the source system.
error.expiredMsg: This deep link has expired. Please request a new link from the source system.
error.notFoundMsg: The linked patient could not be found in the FHIR system. Please verify the patient data in the source system.
error.disabledMsg: Deep linking is not enabled in this installation. Contact your system administrator.
error.replayMsg: This link has already been used. For security reasons, each link can only be opened once. Please request a new link.
error.contactHint: If this problem persists, please contact IT support.

orderNumbers.title: Order Numbers
orderNumbers.subtitle: Org rules, number pool, and alert monitoring
